name: In thee shall all nations be blessed
notes: This weave traces the Abrahamic promise that the nations of the earth would be blessed through Abraham and his seed, given twice in Genesis and cited by both Peter and Paul as fulfilled in Christ. God first speaks it to Abraham as a blessing through him (Gen 12:3, 'in thee'), then renews it after the binding of Isaac as a blessing through his seed (Gen 22:18, 'in thy seed'). Peter, preaching in the temple, quotes the seed-form to the children of the covenant (Acts 3:25), while Paul cites the promise as the gospel preached beforehand unto Abraham (Gal 3:8) and then identifies the singular seed as Christ Himself (Gal 3:16). The two Genesis forms are complementary vantages of one promise, the 'thee' and the 'seed' held together, and the New Testament writers read them as one. Each account keeps its own setting and emphasis: Genesis the covenant moments, Acts the call to repentance, Galatians the doctrine of justification by faith.
